gate_verdicts.reason: Evidence quote ('delta marketplace take rate held steady') names a real cause — the delta marketplace take rate — but the coined name does not. 'unique' is a vague descriptor banned by R7, names no causal variable, and is absent from the source (fails R10's source-noun rule). No coined clean form (e.g. a take-rate name) exists in the catalog to rewrite to, and inventing one is forbidden, so skip.

Evidence ('store foot traffic improved as mall visits recovered') directly supports foot_traffic as a specific, reusable causal noun applicable across retail companies. Clean form, no state/direction/period words, passes R2/R3/R7.

Evidence ('per-order fulfillment costs fell on warehouse automation') names fulfillment cost as the causal variable; 'fell' and the automation mechanism stay in driver_state/evidence. Standard, reusable cost-metric noun; passes all rules.

Evidence ('gamma loyalty program enrollment expanded') names a valid brand-specific cause — gamma loyalty program enrollment — but the coined name 'gamma_unique' does not name it. 'unique' is a vague non-causal descriptor (R7) not found in the source (R10). No coined clean form (e.g. an enrollment name) exists in the catalog to rewrite to, so skip rather than invent a name.

Evidence ('rising crude oil prices lifted our freight and shipping costs') matches the R5 familiar market-driver form oil_price exactly. Highly reusable macro cause; 'rising' correctly excluded from the name.

Evidence ('hourly store wages rose with minimum wage increases') attributes the cause specifically to store-level hourly wages; name follows R3 word order and keeps state ('rose') out. NOT folded with warehouse_hourly_wages — same metric but different scope (store vs warehouse), so the 3-check fails and they stay separate (R9).

Evidence ('warehouse hourly wages climbed amid tight labor markets') attributes the cause specifically to warehouse-level hourly wages; clean reusable noun, state excluded. Kept separate from store_hourly_wages because scope differs (3-check: same metric, different scope).
